messages.content:
The major climate zones of the planet earth are, except
 A. Ocean Zones
 B. Polar Zones
 C. Dry/Arid Zones
 D. Tropical Zones
 E. Temperate Zones
 F. Continental Zones
F. Continental Zones

The major elements to consider in defining climate change include the following, except
 A. Global Warming
 B. Long term changes in precipitation
 C. Changes in the Lithosphere
 D. Long term changes in temperatures
 E. Extreme weather patterns
C. Changes in the Lithosphere

Normal greenhouse effect is important for the sustenance of life on earth as it has increased the earth’s surface temperature by
 A. 33 ℃
 B. 50 ℃
 C. 15 ℃
 D. – 18 ℃

Which of the following can be caused by both natural and human factors
 A. Amounts of greenhouse gases
 B. Volcanic Activity
 C. Pollution
 D. Deforestation
 E. Tectonic Activity
A. Amounts of greenhouse gases

Earth's climate ________.
 A. Will stabilize over the next century, according to the predictions of most scientists
 B. Has been stable over the history of the planet
 C. Has been documented to have changed once due to the evolution of green photosynthesizing plants
 D. Is changing as a result of natural and human processes
 E. History is undeterminable because there is no method of studying climatic history of the planet
D. Is changing as a result of natural and human processes

Which of these greenhouse gases is most abundant in the atmosphere?
 A. Nitrous oxide
 B. Methane
 C. Water vapour
 D. Carbon dioxide
C. Water vapour

Greenhouse gases are
 A. Carbon dioxide, nitrogen oxides, methane and CFC
 B. Carbon dioxide, Sulphur dioxide, CFC and methane
 C. Carbon mono oxide, Carbon dioxide, Methane and Nitrogen
 D. Carbon mono oxide, Sulphur dioxide, Nitric oxide and Methane
A. Carbon dioxide, nitrogen oxides, methane, and CFC

The most important reason for decrease in biodiversity is
 A. Introduction of exotic species
 B. Habitat destruction
 C. Habitat pollution
 D. Tourism
 E. Over- exploitation
B. Habitat destruction

Which of the following contributes the greatest amount of energy related warming
 A. Manufacturing
 B. Electricity
 C. Fugitive Emissions
 D. Other fuel combustion
 E. Transportation
B. Electricity

Gas molecules that absorb thermal infrared radiation and are present in large quantity to alter the climate system is known as
 A. Alpha radiations
 B. Beta radiations
 C. Greenhouse gases
 D. Ozone gases
C. Greenhouse gases
